meta.description: AI or Not? Game Content Library

items.attribution: Photo by Johny Goerend on Unsplash
items.explanation: This is a real photograph of a dramatic aurora borealis. The vivid green and purple colors look surreal, but auroras genuinely produce these colors when solar particles excite nitrogen and oxygen in the atmosphere. The uneven glow and natural noise in the dark sky are clues this is real.

items.attribution: Photo by Samuel Ferrara on Unsplash
items.explanation: This is a real photograph of a mountain landscape. The dramatic scale and lighting can make mountain photos look AI-generated, but notice the natural irregularity in the rock textures and cloud formations — AI tends to smooth these out or repeat patterns.

items.attribution: Photo by Matt Hardy on Unsplash
items.explanation: This is a real photograph of an ocean wave. The translucent water and dynamic spray might look too perfect, but real waves captured at high shutter speeds freeze water in ways that look surreal. Look for the randomness in the droplets — AI struggles to make water spray truly random.

items.attribution: Photo by Boris Smokrovic on Unsplash
items.explanation: This is a real photograph of a colorful bird. The vibrant plumage looks almost too vivid to be real, but many bird species have evolved intensely colorful feathers. Look at the fine detail in individual feathers — AI often makes feathers look painted or blurry at the edges.

items.attribution: Photo by Manja Vitolic on Unsplash
items.explanation: This is a real photograph of a cat. Cats are a common AI subject, but real cat photos have natural imperfections — slightly asymmetric ears, individual whisker detail, and realistic eye reflections. AI cat images often have too-smooth fur or oddly shaped pupils.

items.attribution: Photo by Hiroko Yoshii on Unsplash
items.explanation: This is a real photograph of a jellyfish. The translucent body and flowing tentacles look ethereal, almost like a digital creation. But jellyfish genuinely have this otherworldly appearance — look for the natural light refraction through the bell and the organic randomness in tentacle positions.

items.attribution: Photo by Aaron Burden on Unsplash
items.explanation: This is a real macro photograph of a flower. Macro photography reveals textures and details invisible to the naked eye, which can make subjects look AI-generated. The giveaway: natural imperfections in the petals and pollen grains that AI tends to smooth away.

items.attribution: Photo by Breno Machado on Unsplash
items.explanation: This is a real photograph of a storm. Dramatic weather events like lightning and towering clouds can look unreal, but natural weather has chaotic, unpredictable patterns that AI struggles to replicate. Notice the irregular cloud edges and natural light gradients.

items.attribution: Photo by Aaron Burden on Unsplash
items.explanation: This is a real macro photograph of frost crystals. The geometric patterns in ice look almost too perfect to be natural, but ice crystals genuinely form repeating hexagonal structures. Notice the slight variations and imperfections in each crystal — real physics produces near-symmetry, not perfection.

items.attribution: Photo by Benjamin Voros on Unsplash
items.explanation: This is a real photograph of a starry sky over mountains. Long-exposure photography captures far more stars than the naked eye can see, making the sky look surreal. The giveaway that it's real: natural sensor noise in the dark areas and slight star trailing from Earth's rotation.

This is a real video of a bearded dragon sitting at a miniature dinner table with a bow tie, candle, wine bottle, and framed photos. The scene is an elaborate staged setup with a real reptile — notice the natural eye movements and textured scales that AI video models struggle to replicate convincingly.

This is an AI-generated video of a chihuahua jumping into a pool. It looks convincing at first glance, but watch the water interaction closely — the splash and ripples don't behave quite like real water physics. The dog's paddling motion is also slightly too smooth and repetitive compared to a real dog swimming.

This is an AI-generated video of a squirrel investigating a coffee mug. The scene looks natural with the backyard setting, but the squirrel's movements are subtly too fluid and the way it interacts with the mug edge doesn't show realistic physics. The liquid surface in the mug also lacks natural reflections.

This is a real video of several toads or frogs on a purple mat reacting to a phone screen being shown to them. The natural skin textures, independent eye movements, and unpredictable animal reactions are very difficult for AI to replicate. The hand entering frame with natural motion confirms authenticity.

This is a real video of a fluffy black dog shaking or being playful indoors. The motion blur, natural fur physics, and casual phone-camera quality with a social media watermark are signs of authentic user-generated content. AI video still struggles with realistic fur movement during fast motion.

This is an AI-generated video of a ram headbutting near wooden pallets. The animal's movement and the scene look plausible, but watch the interaction between the ram and the environment — the physics of impact and the wool texture have subtle inconsistencies that reveal it as AI-generated.

This is a real video of an Indian Ringneck parakeet standing on a couch. The bird's natural feather detail, realistic eye reflections, and the casual indoor phone-camera setting are all authentic. AI-generated birds typically have issues with feather layering and natural head movements.

This is an AI-generated video of a kangaroo confronting a Halloween witch decoration. The nighttime setting helps hide imperfections, but the kangaroo's movement is slightly too smooth, and the lighting interaction between the animal and the decorations isn't quite physically consistent.

This is a real video showing a close-up of what appears to be a specialized tool or axe mechanism in a workshop. The natural hand movements, realistic metallic reflections, workshop clutter in the background, and consistent lighting are all signs of authentic footage.

This is a real video of a pit bull in a hoodie appearing to eat noodles with chopsticks (someone's hands are in the sleeves). The dog's natural facial expressions, realistic drool and noodle physics, and the kitchen setting are authentic. This could fool people because the staged setup looks almost too absurd to be real.

This is a real video showing someone's hand near a Nintendo Entertainment System with a Super Mario Bros cartridge, a TV Guide magazine, bubble gum, and other 80s memorabilia. The authentic period details, natural hand movement, and real product labels with legible text are strong indicators of real footage.

This is a real video of a pet raccoon wearing a small green hoodie, sitting in a net hammock and holding a toothpick. The raccoon's natural fur texture, dexterous paw movements, and realistic eye reflections are unmistakably real. Social media watermark visible confirms user-generated content.

This is an AI-generated video showing what appears to be a dog with a shaved head next to a child with clippers. The scene is designed to look like a funny home video, but the dog's facial features have subtle uncanny-valley artifacts, and the fur-to-skin transition on the shaved area doesn't look quite right.

This is a real video showing Chladni patterns — sand or sodium on a vibrating plate forming geometric patterns at different sound frequencies (4221 Hz shown). These patterns are a well-known physics demonstration. The symmetric but slightly imperfect patterns and the visible text overlay are authentic.

This is a real stop-motion animation video of miniature food made from felt/wool. The finger placing a tiny felt ball into a miniature bowl shows real physical interaction. Stop-motion has a distinctive frame-by-frame quality that differs from both regular video and AI generation — each frame is a real photograph.

This is a real video of a miniature model train diorama with autumn foliage, built inside a round wooden container. A hand is adjusting the controls. The incredible detail in the miniature trees and train cars is real craftsmanship, not AI — notice the consistent physical materials and the realistic motion of the train on tracks.

This is real stop-motion animation of a Godzilla toy attacking Peeps marshmallow bunnies. Stop-motion is a filmmaking technique, not AI generation. The physical texture of the toy and candy is consistent with real objects.
This is a real video of a cockatoo interacting with a small dog on a couch. The natural head movements, feather detail, and ambient room lighting are consistent with real phone camera footage.

This is REAL. A parrot owner built a custom underwater capsule to take his bird on diving adventures. The absurdity of the scenario makes people assume it must be AI, but the water physics, light refraction, and bubble behavior are all consistent with real underwater footage. Sometimes reality is stranger than AI.

This is AI-generated. Look for: unnatural motion in the eagle's wings, inconsistent shadows between the baby and eagle, the security-camera timestamp overlay is a stylistic trick to add false authenticity, and the eagle's interaction with the ground plane is physically implausible.

This is real. Wolffish can maintain biting reflexes after decapitation due to their nervous system. The video shows commercial fishermen reacting to this. The gritty boat interior, clothing, and reactive human movements are all consistent with real footage.
This is real footage of a fisherman handling an octopus on a small boat. The ocean waves, boat motion, and octopus tentacle movements are all physically consistent with real-world behavior.
